prompt:
Explain what this Go code does:

func run(jobs <-chan int, wg *sync.WaitGroup){ defer wg.Done(); for j := range jobs { _ = j } }
reference: run is a worker in a worker-pool pattern. It receives jobs from the read-only channel jobs and processes each until the channel is closed and drained, at which point the range loop ends. The deferred wg.Done() signals the WaitGroup that this worker has finished, letting the coordinator wait for all workers via wg.Wait().
prediction: This is a single worker for a worker pool. It ranges over the receive-only channel jobs, handling each value until the channel is closed and drained, which ends the loop. The deferred wg.Done() decrements the WaitGroup counter when the worker returns, so a coordinator calling wg.Wait() can block until every worker has finished.
